text:
Once upon a quiet afternoon in Willow trees town.

There lived a poet owl named Oliver Owlley. Oliver spent his days stealing quiet moments.

One day ollie read old tomes. Birds love owlogy.

He discovered poetry. He wrote. His favorite thing.

He recited ode to owls.

That's owl magic!

It soared.

Nightowl friends came. Owl parties!

You wouldn owl!


You prefer?

Just think how beautiful words might Owlcate!

Sleep tight! Just feathers.



Maybe one owl.


I love owls!

No! Just soft straw!

That owl might feather.

Maybe some feathers.

Soft stuff!

A snowy owl.




Oliver loves snowy owl feathers.

She owls a house.


Just soft!

That one has owlat!





There!

That owl steals stuff!

Maybe an old book!



You owl?


A letter!

No!

Some owl!

Oh!

I got mail!

Oh!

It sounds lovely!

You owl.




Maybe.

Never knew!




You
